prompt:
You are dispatched against the outcome node `.the-valley/outcomes/oc-49555c7-requirements-from-ladder.md` — read it first; it defines this task. This is the first agent dispatch targeting a knowledge node instead of a GitHub issue (an S1 acceptance criterion of design/user-scenarios.md — mention that in the PR description).

## The work: re-derive design/requirements.md from the scenario ladder
Read: design/user-scenarios.md (the ladder — the requirements SOURCE), the current design/requirements.md (predates the ladder; written from the seven-concerns analysis of GitHub), README.md, design/architecture.md (its 'serves:' traceability lines point at requirements — keep them resolvable), and .the-valley/ideas/ida-3145b7a-demand-pressure.md + ida-eac723e-outcome-dag.md (adopted/exploring bets the requirements should not contradict).

Rewrite design/requirements.md so the requirements FALL OUT OF THE RUNGS rather than standing beside them:
- Keep: who it's for, the four constraints with their why, non-goals, durability as first-class. These survive; re-anchor their justifications to rungs where natural (durability ← S1; small-teams trust ← S5; non-goal public-scale ← S7's 'deferred, maybe never').
- Restructure the needs: derive each requirement from the rung(s) that demand it, with an explicit rung reference — e.g. never-losable (S1), integration-latency-in-seconds and trustworthy-local-verification (S2), agents-as-first-class-authors with attribution (S3), causality-queryable-from-one-history (S4), grantable/bounded/revocable trust (S5), attributed-incident-memory (S6). The old seven-concerns framing may survive as a short mapping note (the unbundling analysis), but the rungs are now the organizing spine.
- Also state the demand-shaped work requirement that the outcome-graph experiment implies (work is tracked as outcomes; the system exerts pressure toward roots — one requirement-level sentence, traced to the engine framing in README 'Where this goes', not mechanics).
- Keep it SHORT — this is the requirements layer; a page-ish. No mechanisms.
- Check architecture.md's 'Serves:' lines still point at real requirement names post-rewrite; adjust wording there minimally if a referenced requirement was renamed (do not otherwise touch architecture.md).

## Close the node in the same PR
Edit .the-valley/outcomes/oc-49555c7-requirements-from-ladder.md: status: open → done, and append one line to the body noting completion ('Closed by <this PR>. Requirements now derive from the ladder.'). This exercises open→worked→closed as a node. Do NOT touch other outcome nodes — the scheduler (coordinator session) manages the rest of the graph.

## Constraints
- All relative links resolve. Match voice. Net size of requirements.md should stay roughly flat or smaller.
- PR description: note the dispatch-against-a-node first, then the rewrite summary (old spine → rung-derived spine), and that oc-49555c7 closes with this PR, unblocking oc-2fbcd7b (roadmap re-derivation). No AI/assistant attribution, no Co-Authored-By.

Open a PR.
